mission.title: hami: CUDA_DISABLE_CONTROL=true has no effect — HAMI still hijacks GPU behavior
mission.description: CUDA_DISABLE_CONTROL=true has no effect — HAMI still hijacks GPU behavior. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current hami deployment
steps.description:
Verify your hami version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n hami -l app.kubernetes.io/name=hami
helm list -n hami 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working hami installation.

steps.title: Review hami configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant hami configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n hami -l app.kubernetes.io/name=hami
kubectl get configmap -n hami -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=hami
```
**Description:**

Hi,

I’m currently running a container with the following setup:

* GPU: NVIDIA L40S
* Driver version: 570.124.06
* CUDA version: 12.8

Even when I set `CUDA_DISABLE_CONTROL=true`, I’m seeing that `hami` still hijacks control of

steps.title: Apply the fix for CUDA_DISABLE_CONTROL=true has no effect — HAMI still…
steps.description:
Add a new test case that old code can't pass and the new code can pass.
```yaml
[HAMI-core Msg]: Initializing.....
[HAMI-core Msg]: Calling exit handler
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n hami -l app.kubernetes.io/name=hami
kubectl get events -n hami --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "CUDA_DISABLE_CONTROL=true has no effect — HAMI still…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Add a new test case that old code can't pass and the new code can pass.
codeSnippets:
[HAMI-core Msg]: Initializing.....
[HAMI-core Msg]: Calling exit handler

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: model
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - args:
        - sleep
        - infinity
        env:
        - name: CUDA_DISABLE_CONTROL
          value: "true"
        image: tritonserver:25.04-trtllm-python-py3
        name: triton
        resources:
          limits:
            nvidia.com/gpu: "2"
          requests:
            nvidia.com/gpu: "2"
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /etc/ld.so.preload
          name: hami-preload-blocker
          readOnly: true
          subPath: preload
      initContainers:
      - command:
        - sh
        - -c
        - echo '' > /preload/preload
        image: busybox
        name: hami-preload-blocker
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /preload
          nam

apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: cuda-gpu-pod
spec:
  tolerations:
    - effect: NoSchedule
      key: nvidia.com/gpu
      operator: Exists
  containers:
    - name: busybox
      image: busybox
      command: ["sleep", "infinity"]
    - name: cuda
      image: nvidia/cuda:12.4.1-cudnn-devel-ubuntu22.04
      command: ["/bin/sleep", "infinity"]
      env:
        - name: CUDA_DISABLE_CONTROL
          value: "true"
      resources:
        limits:
          nvidia.com/gpu: "1"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with hami installed or the issue environment reproducible.
